description:
Goal: stop losing ordinary surface programs at the Compiler2 source boundary once control/data lowering is ready.

Signal:
- representative fixtures that currently fail on leftover macros or capture sugar (`&(&1 + &2)`, `defmacro` call sites that should already be expanded) run through `fz2`
- Compiler2 indexing/lowering no longer expects user code to already have skipped macro expansion by magic

Change:
- provide Compiler2 with expanded macro-free function/item bodies, or desugar the remaining surface forms (`Capture`, `CaptureArg`) before body lowering
- keep the ownership boundary honest: Compiler2 receives compiler-owned AST/facts, not old-world runtime execution artifacts

Why:
- even after control/data support lands, real fixtures like `macro_inc` and `lambda_sugars` will still fail unless the frontend surface into Compiler2 is completed
title: Compiler2 frontend surface: feed expanded macro-free bodies and capture sugar
